title: Piper dasyoura
taxonomy: Piper dasyoura is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Piperaceae, which includes pepper plants. It belongs to the genus Piper, a group of plants commonly known as pepper vines. The classification of Piper dasyoura is further supported by its characteristics, such as its woody stems and small, greenish-yellow flowers. Piper dasyoura is native to New Guinea and surrounding areas, and its classification reflects its unique features and geographical distribution. This plant's classification has significant implications for its conservation and potential uses in medicine and other fields.

intro: Piper dasyoura, also known as the Nepalese long pepper or Himalayan long pepper, is a perennial climbing plant native to the Himalayas and parts of Southeast Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in reducing pain, fever, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Piper dasyoura include piperine, piperic acid, and other alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Piper dasyoura is commonly prepared as an infusion, decoction, or tincture, often used to treat various health conditions, including indigestion, diarrhea, and sore throats.
